mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals real economic expansion, which supports corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 forward returns positive over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: When INDPRO rises month-over-month, the 60-day forward SPX return is negative in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A spike in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals recessionary conditions that compress earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In months following UNRATE exceeding 5.5%, the 20-day SPX return is positive in more than 55% of occurrences, or the average return is not statistically different from baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth (RSAFS) is robust and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is low, the joint conditions signal strong consumption supported by easy monetary policy, producing positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the combined signal (interaction term) over 1-week forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant in a linear regression (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates a robust expansion phase, which historically leads to positive S&P 500 returns. This mechanism is expected to be particularly powerful during non-recessionary regimes.
failure_condition: During NBER-defined expansions, the 20-day forward SPX return following GDP-triggered signals is not significantly higher than the unconditional expansion mean (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 5%, the tightening monetary policy raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Over any 3-year rolling window, the 60-day mean SPX return after FEDFUNDS exceeds 5% is non-negative or fails to be statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects broad-based economic momentum from both the industrial and construction sectors, which supports corporate earnings and boosts equity prices over a medium-term horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal's 20-day forward return accuracy (directional hit rate) is below 55% or its Sharpe ratio is less than 0.5 over any 3-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year inflation rate (CPIAUCSL) above 4% forces the Federal Reserve to maintain or raise interest rates, compressing valuations and eroding real earnings, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year exceeds 4%, the 20-day SPX return is positive in more than 60% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) indicates a healthy labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns. The mechanism is expected to be most reliable during employment expansion regimes, not during contractions.
regime_hint: employment expansion (periods with non-negative employment growth)
failure_condition: During employment expansion phases, the 60-day forward SPX return following the trigger is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's hit rate is below 55% over any 5-year window.
